text: Planning a trip is always exciting, but traveling itself teaches you things no guidebook can. When I visited Japan last year, I carefully planned my route from Tokyo to Kyoto, yet the best moments were unexpected. I explored new cultures by wandering through ancient temples and bustling markets. The food was incredible — from fresh sushi to steaming bowls of ramen — but ordering was tricky due to language barriers. I often pointed at menus or used hand gestures. Despite the communication challenges, those encounters led to warm smiles from locals. Now, the strongest memories are not of famous sights, but of small, human connections that shaped my journey.
